---
title: "Interactive Visualization with R Shiny"
subtitle: "PLSC 498 - Week 15, Lecture 2: Advanced Layouts"
author: "Jared Edgerton"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: [default, metropolis, metropolis-fonts, plsc498.css]
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      slideNumberFormat: ""
---

# Lecture 2: Advanced Shiny

Today we'll explore different approaches to building Shiny applications:

1. **Navigation Bar Layout** - Multi-section apps with top navigation
2. **Shinydashboard** - Professional dashboard framework
3. **bslib** - Modern Bootstrap 5 theming
4. **Case Study:** Correlates of War interactive app

---

# Approach 2: Navigation Bar Layout

## When to Use `navbarPage()`

```r
navbarPage(
  title = "My App Title",
  tabPanel("Home", "Content here"),
  tabPanel("Analysis", "More content"),
  tabPanel("Data", "Table here"),
  tabPanel("About", "Information")
)
```

**Best for:**
- Apps with multiple distinct sections
- Logical flow or workflow
- Clear hierarchical organization
- 3-6 main sections

**vs Sidebar Layout:**
- More scalable for many controls
- Better for complex analyses
- Cleaner navigation

---

# navbarPage Example: Senate Explorer

```r
library(shiny)

ui <- navbarPage(
  title = "Senate Explorer",
  theme = bslib::bs_theme(primary = "#003366"),

  # Main visualization
  tabPanel(
    "Visualization",
    br(),
    fluidRow(
      column(3,
        h4("Controls"),
        selectInput("congress", "Congress:",
                    choices = unique(senate_data$congress)),
        checkboxGroupInput("parties", "Parties:",
                          choices = c("D", "R"), selected = c("D", "R"))
      ),
      column(9,
        plotOutput("main_plot", height = "600px")
      )
    )
  ),

  # Statistics tab
  tabPanel(
    "Statistics",
    h4("Summary Statistics"),
    fluidRow(
      column(6, tableOutput("party_stats")),
      column(6, plotOutput("distribution"))
    )
  ),

  # Data explorer
  tabPanel(
    "Data",
    downloadButton("download", "Download CSV"),
    dataTableOutput("table")
  ),

  # About page
  tabPanel(
    "About",
    h3("About This App"),
    p("This app explores US Senate ideology scores...")
  )
)
```

---

# navbarPage Structure

**Key Components:**

1. **Title** - appears in navbar
2. **Theme** - optional Bootstrap theme
3. **Tab Panels** - each tab in navbar
4. **Nested Layouts** - use `fluidRow()` and `column()` within tabs

**Server logic same as before:**
```r
server <- function(input, output) {
  filtered <- reactive({
    senate_data %>%
      filter(congress == input$congress,
             party %in% input$parties)
  })

  output$main_plot <- renderPlot({ /* plot */ })
  output$party_stats <- renderTable({ /* table */ })
  output$table <- renderDataTable({ filtered() })
  output$download <- downloadHandler(...)
}
```

---

# Approach 3: Shinydashboard

## Dashboard Framework for Professional Layouts

```r
library(shinydashboard)

ui <- dashboardPage(
  # Header with title
  dashboardHeader(title = "Analytics Dashboard"),

  # Left sidebar with menu
  dashboardSidebar(
    sidebarMenu(
      menuItem("Dashboard", tabName = "dashboard"),
      menuItem("Reports", tabName = "reports"),
      menuItem("Settings", tabName = "settings")
    )
  ),

  # Main content area
  dashboardBody(
    tabItems(
      tabItem(tabName = "dashboard",
        # Dashboard content
      ),
      tabItem(tabName = "reports",
        # Reports content
      )
    )
  )
)
```

**Best for:**
- Professional dashboards
- Multiple sections with sidebar menu
- Business/admin interfaces
- KPI monitoring

---

# shinydashboard: Value Boxes

Display key metrics with **value boxes**:

```r
# In dashboardBody or tabItem
fluidRow(
  valueBox(
    value = 45,
    subtitle = "Total Conflicts",
    icon = icon("crosshairs"),
    color = "red"
  ),
  valueBox(
    value = 12500,
    subtitle = "Total Fatalities",
    icon = icon("heartbeat"),
    color = "maroon"
  ),
  valueBox(
    value = 32,
    subtitle = "Countries Involved",
    icon = icon("flag"),
    color = "blue"
  )
)
```

Creates highlighted metric boxes at the top of dashboard.

---

# shinydashboard: Boxes for Content

```r
# Regular boxes for plots/tables
fluidRow(
  box(
    title = "Conflict Timeline",
    plotOutput("timeline"),
    width = 8,
    height = 500
  ),
  box(
    title = "War Type Distribution",
    plotOutput("wartype_pie"),
    width = 4,
    height = 500
  )
)

# Box with collapsible/closable options
box(
  title = "Advanced Options",
  "Content here",
  collapsible = TRUE,
  collapsed = TRUE,
  closable = TRUE
)
```

---

# shinydashboard: Complete Example

```r
library(shiny)
library(shinydashboard)

ui <- dashboardPage(
  dashboardHeader(title = "Conflicts Dashboard"),

  dashboardSidebar(
    sliderInput("year_range", "Year Range:",
                min = 1950, max = 2020, value = c(1980, 2020)),
    checkboxGroupInput("war_type", "War Type:",
                      choices = c("Inter-state", "Intra-state"))
  ),

  dashboardBody(
    fluidRow(
      valueBoxOutput("total_conflicts"),
      valueBoxOutput("total_deaths"),
      valueBoxOutput("countries_involved")
    ),
    fluidRow(
      box(plotOutput("timeline"), width = 12)
    ),
    fluidRow(
      box(plotOutput("by_type"), width = 6),
      box(tableOutput("top_countries"), width = 6)
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output) {
  filtered <- reactive({
    cow_data %>%
      filter(year >= input$year_range[1],
             year <= input$year_range[2],
             war_type %in% input$war_type)
  })

  output$total_conflicts <- renderValueBox({
    valueBox(nrow(filtered()), "Total Conflicts")
  })
  # ... more outputs
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

---

# Approach 4: bslib for Modern Design

## Bootstrap 5 Theming with `bslib`

bslib provides modern, responsive design:

```r
library(bslib)

theme <- bs_theme(
  version = 5,
  primary = "#1f77b4",
  secondary = "#d62728",
  success = "#2ca02c",
  base_font = font_google("Roboto")
)

ui <- page_navbar(
  title = "Modern Dashboard",
  theme = theme,

  nav_panel(
    "Dashboard",
    layout_sidebar(
      sidebar = sidebar("Filter controls"),
      navset_card_tab(
        nav_panel("Overview", "Content"),
        nav_panel("Details", "More content")
      )
    )
  )
)
```

**Advantages:**
- ✓ Modern Bootstrap 5 design
- ✓ Responsive on mobile
- ✓ Beautiful default styling
- ✓ Easy color customization

---

# bslib Components

## `page_navbar()` - Top Navigation

```r
page_navbar(
  title = "My App",
  nav_panel("Home", "Welcome"),
  nav_panel("Analysis", "Results"),
  nav_panel("Data", "Download")
)
```

## `layout_sidebar()` - Sidebar Layout

```r
layout_sidebar(
  sidebar = sidebar(
    sliderInput("n", "N:", 1, 100, 50)
  ),
  # Main content
  plotOutput("plot")
)
```

## `navset_card_tab()` - Tabbed Cards

```r
navset_card_tab(
  nav_panel("Tab 1", "Content 1"),
  nav_panel("Tab 2", "Content 2"),
  nav_panel("Tab 3", "Content 3")
)
```

## `value_box()` - Metric Display

```r
value_box(
  title = "Total Revenue",
  value = "$1.2M",
  theme = "primary"
)
```

---

# bslib Color System

Pre-defined color themes (like Bootstrap):

```r
# Create custom theme
theme <- bs_theme(
  version = 5,
  primary = "#1f77b4",      # Main color
  secondary = "#d62728",    # Accent
  success = "#2ca02c",      # Positive
  danger = "#ff7f0e",       # Warning/Negative
  info = "#17a2b8",         # Informational
  warning = "#ffc107",      # Caution
  light = "#f8f9fa",        # Light background
  dark = "#343a40"          # Dark text
)

# Use in ui
ui <- page_navbar(theme = theme, ...)

# Apply to components
value_box(value = 42, theme = "success")
card(card_header("Title"), theme = "primary")
```

---

# bslib Full App Example

```r
library(shiny)
library(bslib)

theme <- bs_theme(
  version = 5,
  primary = "#1f77b4",
  secondary = "#d62728"
)

ui <- page_navbar(
  title = "Conflict Explorer",
  theme = theme,

  nav_panel(
    "Dashboard",
    layout_sidebar(
      sidebar = sidebar(
        sliderInput("year", "Year:", 1950, 2020, c(1980, 2020)),
        checkboxGroupInput("type", "Type:", c("Inter-state", "Intra-state"))
      ),
      layout_column_wrap(
        value_box("45", "Total Conflicts", theme = "primary"),
        value_box("12,500", "Deaths", theme = "danger"),
        value_box("32", "Countries", theme = "success"),
        col_widths = c(4, 4, 4)
      ),
      br(),
      card(
        full_screen = TRUE,
        card_header("Timeline"),
        plotOutput("timeline")
      )
    )
  ),

  nav_panel(
    "Data",
    dataTableOutput("table")
  )
)
```

---

# Comparison of Approaches

| Aspect | Sidebar | navbarPage | shinydashboard | bslib |
|--------|---------|-----------|----------------|-------|
| Learning Curve | Easy | Easy | Medium | Medium |
| Customization | Good | Good | Limited | Excellent |
| Modern Look | Basic | Good | Professional | Excellent |
| Mobile Responsive | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Best For | Simple apps | Multi-section | Dashboards | Modern apps |
| Setup Time | Fast | Fast | Medium | Fast |
| Documentation | Excellent | Good | Good | Growing |

---

# Correlates of War Dataset

**What is COW?**
- Comprehensive database of interstate conflicts since 1816
- Key variables: duration, fatalities, war type, participants
- Public data from https://correlatesofwar.org/

**Our Data:**
- Recent conflicts (1945-2020)
- Interstate wars only
- Variables: country, year, war number, type, duration, deaths

---

# COW App: shinydashboard Approach

The shinydashboard version provides:

1. **Header with KPI boxes:**
   - Total conflicts
   - Total fatalities
   - Average duration
   - Countries involved

2. **Sidebar filtering:**
   - Year range slider
   - War type checkboxes
   - Country selector

3. **Tabbed analysis:**
   - Overview: timeline + distributions
   - Conflicts: fatalities, duration, initiator patterns
   - Countries: participation rankings
   - Data: raw data explorer + download

---

# COW App: bslib Approach

Modern design using Bootstrap 5:

1. **Top navigation with 4 sections:**
   - Dashboard (main overview)
   - Countries (country-specific analysis)
   - Data (explorer + download)
   - About (information)

2. **Responsive cards:**
   - Full-screen toggle for visualizations
   - Modern color scheme
   - Smooth interactions

3. **Interactive elements:**
   - Plotly for tooltips and hover
   - Country selection for detailed view
   - Multiple visualization types

---

# Building Your Own App: Best Practices

**1. Start Simple**
- Begin with basic sidebar layout
- Add features incrementally
- Test frequently

**2. Organize Code**
- Put UI and server in separate sections
- Use comments to mark sections
- Create reactive expressions for reuse

**3. Performance**
- Cache expensive computations
- Use `reactive()` for shared data
- Filter early, visualize later

**4. User Experience**
- Clear labels and instructions
- Sensible defaults
- Loading indicators for slow operations

**5. Deployment**
- Test locally first with `shiny::runApp()`
- Consider shinyapps.io or Shiny Server
- Document dependencies in requirements.txt

---

# Example: Adding Interactivity

```r
# Basic: static visualization
output$plot <- renderPlot({
  ggplot(data, aes(x, y)) + geom_point()
})

# Better: filtering based on input
output$plot <- renderPlot({
  filtered <- data %>%
    filter(year >= input$year_min,
           year <= input$year_max)

  ggplot(filtered, aes(x, y)) +
    geom_point(color = input$color)
})

# Advanced: multiple reactive steps
filtered <- reactive({
  data %>%
    filter(year >= input$year_min,
           year <= input$year_max,
           type %in% input$type)
})

summarized <- reactive({
  filtered() %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    summarize(mean_value = mean(value))
})

output$plot <- renderPlot({
  ggplot(summarized(), aes(region, mean_value)) +
    geom_col()
})

output$table <- renderTable({
  summarized()
})
```

---

# Common Advanced Patterns

## Pattern 1: Conditional Tabs
```r
output$ui <- renderUI({
  if (input$data_type == "senate") {
    tabsetPanel(
      tabPanel("Ideology Plot", plotOutput("ideology")),
      tabPanel("Party Stats", tableOutput("stats"))
    )
  } else {
    tabsetPanel(
      tabPanel("Conflict Timeline", plotOutput("timeline")),
      tabPanel("Deaths by Type", plotOutput("deaths"))
    )
  }
})
```

## Pattern 2: Downloadable Reports
```r
output$download <- downloadHandler(
  filename = function() {
    paste0("report_", Sys.Date(), ".csv")
  },
  content = function(file) {
    write.csv(filtered_data(), file)
  }
)
```

## Pattern 3: Reactive Dependent Inputs
```r
observe({
  # When year changes, update available countries
  filtered_countries <- data %>%
    filter(year == input$year) %>%
    pull(country) %>%
    unique()

  updateSelectInput(session, "country",
                   choices = filtered_countries)
})
```

---

# Debugging Shiny Apps

**Common Issues:**

1. **"object not found"**
   - Missing `library()` call
   - Typo in variable name
   - Check RStudio error message

2. **Reactive not updating**
   - Forgot parentheses: `filtered()` not `filtered`
   - Input name mismatch with input$name

3. **Slow performance**
   - Compute-intensive operations in reactive
   - Use `shiny::isolate()` to break reactivity
   - Cache results with `reactive({...})`

4. **App won't run**
   - Syntax error (check console)
   - Missing closing parenthesis
   - Look at RStudio's error highlighting

**RStudio Debugging:**
- Check console for error messages
- Add `cat()` or `print()` in server
- Use `browser()` to pause execution

---

# Key Takeaways

1. **Multiple layout approaches** - choose based on complexity
2. **navbarPage** - for multi-section apps
3. **shinydashboard** - for professional dashboards
4. **bslib** - for modern, responsive design
5. **Reactive programming** is key to all approaches
6. **COW and Senate apps** demonstrate different styles
7. **Start simple** and add complexity incrementally

---

# Deployment Options

## Local Sharing
```r
shiny::runApp("path/to/app")
# Others access via your IP: http://your.ip:8100
```

## shinyapps.io (Easiest)
```r
# First time:
library(rsconnect)
rsconnect::deployApp("path/to/app")

# App hosted at your-account.shinyapps.io/appname
```

## Shiny Server
- Self-hosted on Linux server
- Full control, more setup required
- Academic licenses available

## Docker
```dockerfile
FROM rocker/shiny:latest
# Copy app files
COPY app.R /srv/shiny-server/app/app.R
```

---

# Resources and Next Steps

**Official Shiny Resources:**
- [Shiny Gallery](https://shiny.rstudio.com/gallery/)
- [Shiny Articles](https://shiny.rstudio.com/articles/)
- [shinydashboard docs](https://rstudio.github.io/shinydashboard/)
- [bslib docs](https://rstudio.github.io/bslib/)

**Our Course Files:**
- `senate_app_01_basic.R` - Sidebar layout
- `senate_app_02_navbar.R` - Navigation bar
- `cow_app_03_dashboard.R` - Shinydashboard
- `cow_app_04_bslib.R` - Modern bslib design

**Practice:**
- Modify apps with different data
- Create your own visualizations
- Deploy to shinyapps.io (free tier)

---

# Final Project Ideas

**Using Shiny:**

1. **Exploratory Data Analysis** app for your research
2. **Interactive policy simulator** showing outcomes
3. **Data dashboard** for public datasets
4. **Educational tool** for statistical concepts
5. **Competition tracker** with real-time updates

All can be deployed and shared with stakeholders!
